raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the cleanup be (A) light copyediting for clarity/conciseness without changing claims or structure, (B) a deeper edit that may adjust framing, claims, and local section ordering to tighten logic and tone, or (C) both A and a pass to soften/qualify potentially overstrong claims (e.g., \"preserving theoretical guarantees\" and \"no manual tuning is required\")?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "“Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polishing for clarity, concision, and flow.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should ensure appropriate signaling of uncertainty around stability, guarantees, and robustness claims.",
    "RX.2": "Cleanup should avoid overstating contributions or certainty (e.g., absolute claims about guarantees or zero tuning).",
    "RX.3": "Work must remain within the scope of a cleanup rather than introducing new technical content or claims.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should not inadvertently encourage misleading or risky interpretations about the method’s reliability or deployment.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should be traceable to the original text so reviewers can verify what was modified and why."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the cleanup be (A) light copyediting for clarity/conciseness without changing claims or structure, (B) a deeper edit that may adjust framing, claims, and local section ordering to tighten logic and tone, or (C) both A and a pass to soften/qualify potentially overstrong claims (e.g., "preserving theoretical guarantees" and "no manual tuning is required")?

rationale.R1.6: “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polishing for clarity, concision, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should ensure appropriate signaling of uncertainty around stability, guarantees, and robustness claims.
rationale.RX.2: Cleanup should avoid overstating contributions or certainty (e.g., absolute claims about guarantees or zero tuning).
rationale.RX.3: Work must remain within the scope of a cleanup rather than introducing new technical content or claims.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should not inadvertently encourage misleading or risky interpretations about the method’s reliability or deployment.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should be traceable to the original text so reviewers can verify what was modified and why.
